text: Databases are essential for storing and organizing data. The two main types are SQL and NoSQL databases. SQL databases, like MySQL, use tables made of rows and columns, similar to spreadsheets. Data modeling in SQL requires careful planning of table relationships. NoSQL databases, such as MongoDB, are more flexible, storing data in documents or graphs without fixed structures. To retrieve information, you use queries. In SQL, this involves writing statements like SELECT * FROM customers. Indexes are special data structures that speed up these queries by allowing the database to find information quickly, much like an index in a book helps you locate a topic.
